item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents consistently oppose giving society automatic priority over individual rights. Agent A repeatedly frames public-interest language as a possible cover for coercion, minority suppression, or abuse of power in Indonesia. Agent B uses a similar U.S. civil-liberties frame and argues that any rights restriction must be narrow, accountable, evidence-based, and challengeable. Both concede that public safety/order can justify limited restrictions, so firmness is high but not absolute. No visible flip or meaningful position movement appears in the debate text.
